name: Colorado SB 24-205 (AI Consumer Protections)
description: Colorado state law regulating high-risk AI systems making consequential decisions affecting consumers. Effective February 1, 2026.
verification_note: NEEDS_OWNER_REVIEW -- MATERIAL FINDING: SB 24-205 never took effect. Its effective date slipped twice (originally 2026-02-01; postponed to 2026-06-30 by SB 25B-004, signed 2025-08-28) and it was then repealed outright before reaching that delayed date. Governor Polis signed SB 26-189 on 2026-05-14, repealing SB 24-205 and replacing it with a narrower disclosure-and-rights framework for automated decision-making technology, effective 2027-01-01. The replacement law drops the duty of care against algorithmic discrimination, the deployer risk-management-program and impact-assessment obligations, and some reporting duties that this dataset's tiers/categories/required_controls below describe -- i.e., the substantive content in this file describes a repealed statute, not current or forthcoming law. Do not cite this dataset's tiers/controls to a client as current Colorado law. A new dataset version modeling SB 26-189's actual disclosure-and-rights framework is needed before this framework is relied on again, and must be authored from the enacted bill text, not secondary summaries. As of this note's verification date, no such version exists. Verified via web search 2026-07-09. Sources: Akin Gump, 'Colorado Postpones Implementation of Colorado AI Act, SB 24-205' (https://www.akingump.com/en/insights/ai-law-and-regulation-tracker/colorado-postpones-implementation-of-colorado-ai-act-sb-24-205); Norton Rose Fulbright, 'Colorado enacts revised AI law' (https://www.nortonrosefulbright.com/en-us/knowledge/publications/18733d31/colorado-enacts-revised-ai-law); McDermott Will & Emery, 'Colorado AI law in flux' (https://www.mcdermottlaw.com/insights/colorado-ai-law-in-flux-comprehensive-replacement-bill-signed-after-federal-court-blocks-predecessors-enforcement/); Seyfarth Shaw, 'Colorado Enacts Artificial Intelligence Replacement Law' (https://www.seyfarth.com/news-insights/colorado-enacts-artificial-intelligence-replacement-law.html).

criteria.summary: AI systems that make, or are a substantial factor in making, a consequential decision affecting consumers.

consequential_decision_categories.employment: Employment or employment opportunity (hiring, firing, promotion, terms of compensation)
consequential_decision_categories.financial_lending: Financial or lending services (loan approval, credit, interest rate determination)

criteria.substantial_factor_definition: An AI system is a 'substantial factor' if it: (a) assists in making a consequential decision; (b) is capable of altering the outcome of the decision; (c) is used to generate any content, decision, prediction, or recommendation related to the consumer.

criteria.summary: AI systems not making consequential decisions, or that fall under specific exemptions.
exemptions: AI systems used solely for research
Systems compliant with federal law providing equivalent protection
